-{"id":"gt-fly0","title":"bd close --continue: auto-advance to next molecule step","description":"Add --continue flag to bd close for seamless molecule step transitions.

## Usage

bd close <step-id> --continue [--no-auto]

## Behavior

1. Closes the specified step
2. Finds next ready step in same molecule (sibling/child)
3. By default, marks it in_progress (--no-auto to skip)
4. Outputs the transition

## Output

[checkmark] Closed gt-abc.3: Implement feature

Next ready in molecule:
  gt-abc.4: Write tests

[arrow] Marked in_progress (use --no-auto to skip)

## If no next step

[checkmark] Closed gt-abc.6: Exit decision

Molecule gt-abc complete! All steps closed.
Consider: bd mol squash gt-abc --summary '...'

## Key behaviors
- Detects parent molecule from closed step
- Finds next unblocked sibling
- Auto-claims by default (propulsion principle)
- Graceful handling when molecule is complete

## Beads feature
This is a bd command - needs implementation in beads repo.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T17:01:00.437929-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T17:01:00.437929-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-frs","title":"Polecat name pooling: Bounded reusable names","description":"Polecats reuse names from a bounded pool (50) with overflow to sequence numbers.

## Naming Scheme
- Pool: polecat-01 through polecat-50 (prefer low numbers)
- Overflow: <rigname>-<sequenceNumber> (e.g., beads-51, gastown-52)

## Design
- Witness tracks which pool names are in use
- On spawn: pick first available from pool
- If pool exhausted: use rigname-N format
- On completion: pool name returns, sequence numbers don't

## Why?
- User experience: tmux sessions survive polecat restarts
- Users stay attached, see new polecat start (like mayor respawn loop)
- Bounded resource usage for common case
- Scales beyond 50 when needed

## Implementation
- Witness maintains name allocation in beads or local state
- Tmux session runs respawn loop (like mayor)
- Name released on graceful exit or when witness detects dead session","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:32:28.43866-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T17:22:52.551244-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T16:29:30.648439-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-odvf","title":"Document bd mol bond/squash/burn CLI","description":"Create CLI reference documentation for molecule commands:

## bd mol bond

Instantiate a proto into a runnable molecule.

```bash
bd mol bond <proto-id> [--wisp] [--assignee=<addr>]
```

- Default: creates a Mol (durable, in main beads)
- --wisp: creates a Wisp (ephemeral, in .beads-ephemeral/)
- --assignee: who will execute this molecule

## bd mol squash

Complete a molecule and generate digest.

```bash
bd mol squash <mol-id> --summary='...'
```

- For Mol: creates digest in git history
- For Wisp: evaporates (no permanent record)
- --summary: required summary of what was accomplished

## bd mol burn

Abandon a molecule without completing.

```bash
bd mol burn <mol-id> [--reason='...']
```

- Discards molecule state
- No digest created
- Use when molecule is no longer needed","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:33:06.462105-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:20:42.829495-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-odvf","depends_on_id":"gt-62hm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:33:17.530156-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-oiv0","title":"Remove bd sync instruction from polecat startup workflow","description":"Polecats are instructed to run `bd sync --from-main` on startup (spawn.go:634).

## Problem
- Spawn command already syncs beads before spawning (line 239)
- Polecats share rig-level beads via `.beads/redirect`
- Multiple polecats starting simultaneously all try to sync same shared beads
- This causes git conflicts/failures when many polecats spawn at once

## Observed
User reported: 'all polecats failing on beads sync on startup in one run'

## Fix
Remove line 634 from buildWorkAssignmentMail():
```
body.WriteString("2. Run \`bd sync --from-main\` to get fresh beads\n")
```

Polecats only need to sync at END of work (already in steps 5/7).

## Files
- internal/cmd/spawn.go: buildWorkAssignmentMail() and buildSpawnContext()","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:52.25177-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:52.25177-08:00"}

-{"id":"gt-ooz6","title":"bd close hooks: context check and notifications","description":"Add hook system to bd close for context checking and notifications.

## Concept

Register hooks that run on every bd close:

# .beads/config.yaml
hooks:
  on_close:
    - command: 'gt context-check'
    - notify: 'Next step ready'

## Use cases

### Context check on close
After closing a step, check if context is getting full.
If >80%, output warning suggesting session cycling.

### Next step notification  
Automatically show next ready step (complements --continue).

### Custom actions
User-defined scripts for workflow automation.

## Hook types

- command: Run shell command with env vars (BEAD_ID, BEAD_TITLE, etc)
- notify: Send message to agent
- webhook: POST to URL (future)

## Integration with context detection

The context-check hook could:
1. Capture tmux pane (if in tmux session)
2. Estimate context usage (turn count, output length)
3. If high, output: 'Context at ~85%. Consider cycling.'

## Priority
P2 - nice to have, not blocking launch.

## Related
- gt-qswb (bd mol current)
- gt-fly0 (bd close --continue)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T17:01:44.717704-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T17:01:44.717704-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-op78","title":"Session cycling: Auto-spawn successor when context full","description":"Enable automatic session succession for crew workers.

When a crew worker's context fills up (>80%), they should:
1. Write handoff mail to themselves
2. Signal session end
3. System auto-spawns successor session
4. Successor reads handoff, finds attached work, auto-continues

This completes the autonomous overnight work pattern from gt-9g82.

Depends on:
- Detecting context fullness (Claude Code API?)
- Session spawning mechanism (tmux/daemon)
- Handoff protocol already implemented","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T16:18:27.570996-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T16:18:27.570996-08:00"}

Expected: Polecat sessions should start for each worker.

 {"id":"gt-ov2","title":"Refinery agent: merge queue processing loop","description":"The Refinery agent processes the merge queue, merging polecat work to main.

## Interface

```go
type Refinery struct {
    rig    *Rig
    queue  *MergeQueue
    git    *Git
    mail   *Mailbox
    config RefineryConfig
}

type RefineryConfig struct {
    AutoMerge     bool          // Auto-merge passing MRs
    RunTests      bool          // Run tests before merge
    TestCommand   string        // Command to run tests
    RequireReview bool          // Require review before merge (future)
}

func NewRefinery(rig *Rig, ...) *Refinery

// Lifecycle
func (r *Refinery) Start() error
func (r *Refinery) Stop() error
func (r *Refinery) IsRunning() bool

// Processing
func (r *Refinery) ProcessQueue() error
func (r *Refinery) ProcessMR(mr *MergeRequest) error
```

## Processing Loop

1. Check queue for pending MRs
2. For each pending MR:
   a. Fetch polecat branch
   b. Attempt merge to refinery/rig (local main)
   c. Run tests if configured
   d. If pass: push to origin, mark merged
   e. If fail: mark rejected, notify polecat
3. Sleep, repeat

## Merge Strategy

- Fast-forward when possible
- Merge commit when not
- On conflict: reject MR, polecat must rebase

## Test Integration

If tests configured:
```bash
cd refinery/rig
git merge polecat/branch
<test_command>  # e.g., go test ./...
```
Result determines merge/reject.

## Notifications

On merge success:
- Mail to polecat: "Your work merged"
- Update bead if issue tracked

On merge failure:
- Mail to polecat: "Merge failed: <reason>"
- Include conflict details if applicable

## Dependencies

Needs: Rig management, Git wrapper, Mail system, Merge queue","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:08.498771-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T14:20:35.032221-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T14:20:35.032221-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-ov2","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:21.801826-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-ov2","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:21.89716-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-qsvq","title":"gt doctor: Detect and clean up orphaned sessions/processes","description":"## Problem
Orphaned tmux sessions and Claude processes accumulate over time, consuming memory.

## Discovered Orphans
- gt-deacon: Idle Claude session, not part of any rig

## Proposed Solution

### gt doctor --check (default)
Detect issues without fixing:
- Orphan sessions (not matching rig/polecat/crew/witness/refinery/mayor pattern)
- Claude processes without parent tmux session
- Tmux sessions without Claude (stuck at bash prompt)
- Polecats marked 'working' but session idle

### gt doctor --fix
Clean up detected issues:
- Kill orphan sessions
- Kill orphan Claude processes
- Optionally reset stuck polecat state

### gt gc (alternative name)
Short alias for cleanup operations.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detects orphan sessions
- [ ] Detects orphan processes
- [ ] Safe cleanup (doesn't kill active work)
- [ ] Reports what was cleaned","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T01:06:39.046959-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T07:54:51.210265-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T07:54:51.210265-08:00"}
-{"id":"gt-qswb","title":"bd mol current: soft cursor showing current/next step","description":"Add bd mol current command for molecule navigation orientation.

## Usage

bd mol current [mol-id]

If mol-id given, show status for that molecule.
If not given, infer from in_progress issues assigned to current agent.

## Output

You're working on molecule gt-abc (Feature X)

  [checkmark] gt-abc.1: Design
  [checkmark] gt-abc.2: Scaffold  
  [checkmark] gt-abc.3: Implement
  [arrow] gt-abc.4: Write tests [in_progress] <- YOU ARE HERE
  [circle] gt-abc.5: Documentation

 {"id":"gt-rm3","title":"CLI: gt refinery commands (start, stop, status, queue)","description":"CLI commands for managing the Refinery agent.

## Commands

```bash
gt refinery start <rig>      # Start refinery for a rig
gt refinery stop <rig>       # Stop refinery
gt refinery status <rig>     # Show refinery status
gt refinery queue <rig>      # Show merge queue
```

## gt refinery start

Starts the Refinery daemon for the specified rig.

Options:
- --foreground: Run in foreground (default: background)
- --auto-merge: Enable auto-merge (default: from config)

## gt refinery stop

Stops a running Refinery. Gracefully finishes current MR if processing.

## gt refinery status

Shows:
- Running state (running/stopped)
- Current MR being processed (if any)
- Queue length
- Last merge time
- Recent activity

## gt refinery queue

Shows the merge queue:
```
Merge queue for 'wyvern':
  1. [pending] Toast/polecat-auth-fix (15m ago)
  2. [pending] Capable/polecat-new-feature (5m ago)
  
1 merged today, 0 rejected
```

## Implementation

Uses gt-ov2 (Refinery agent) for daemon functionality.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:24.754361-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T14:16:34.593637-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T14:16:34.593637-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-rm3","depends_on_id":"gt-ov2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:30.679909-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-rp0k","title":"Extend auto-continue to polecats (not just crew)","description":"gt prime currently only outputs AUTO-CONTINUE MODE for crew workers.
Polecats with attached work should also auto-continue.

## Current behavior
outputCrewAttachmentStatus() in prime.go:
- Only runs for RoleCrew
- Outputs '→ AUTO-CONTINUE MODE' when attached work detected

## Desired behavior
- Rename to outputAttachmentStatus() or similar
- Run for RoleCrew AND RolePolecat
- Same directive: if attachment exists, work immediately

## The Propulsion Principle
'If you find something on your hook, YOU RUN IT.'

This applies to ALL workers, not just crew.

## Implementation
1. Extend role check in outputCrewAttachmentStatus()
2. Adjust assignee detection for polecats vs crew
3. Test with both worker types","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T16:43:22.149252-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T16:43:22.149252-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-s3m0","title":"gt polecat: add 'done' or 'finish' command to transition from working to idle","description":"When a polecat finishes work but session wasn't properly cleaned up, there's no way to reset it from 'working' state back to 'idle'.

Tried:
```
gt polecat sleep gastown/Angharad
Error: sleeping polecat: polecat is not active (state: working)
```

The sleep command only works on 'active' polecats, not 'working' ones.

Had to manually edit state.json to reset:
```
jq '.state = "idle" | .issue = ""' state.json
```

Need a command like:
```
gt polecat done gastown/Angharad   # working -> idle
gt polecat finish gastown/Angharad # working -> idle  
gt polecat reset gastown/Angharad  # any state -> idle (force)
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-19T01:41:39.851037-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T01:57:17.031611-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T01:57:17.031611-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-u818","title":"Witness Plugin System","description":"Plugin infrastructure for witness patrol customization.

## Plugin Location
`<rig>/plugins/witness/<plugin-name>/`

## Plugin Structure
```
plugin.yaml   # Config: name, description, trigger, tier
prompt.md     # The prompt to run
```

## plugin.yaml Schema
```yaml
name: plugin-name
description: What it does
trigger: patrol          # When to run (patrol, on-nudge, on-shutdown)
tier: haiku              # Model tier (haiku, sonnet, opus)
input: polecat-list      # What context to gather (polecat-list, session-capture, etc)
```

## Execution
1. Witness reads plugins from directory
2. For each plugin matching current trigger:
   - Gather specified input context
   - Run prompt.md against specified tier
   - Parse output for gt commands
   - Execute commands

## CLI
- `gt plugin list <rig>` - List installed plugins
- `gt plugin run <rig> <plugin>` - Manual run","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:16:53.886931-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:16:53.886931-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-u82","title":"Design: Mayor session cycling and handoff","description":"Design for Mayor session cycling and structured handoff.

## Overview

Mayor coordinates across all rigs and runs for extended periods. Needs session cycling pattern with structured handoff notes.

## Key Elements

1. Session cycling recognition (when to cycle)
2. Handoff note format (structured state capture)
3. Handoff delivery (mail to self)
4. Fresh session startup (reading and resuming)

## Subtasks (implementation)

- gt-g2d: Mayor session cycling prompting
- gt-sye: Mayor startup protocol prompting
- gt-vci: Mayor handoff mail template
- gt-1le: town handoff command (optional, P2)

**Design complete.** Each subtask has full specification in its description.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:03:16.125725-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:49:26.203276-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-15T20:16:10.772149-08:00"}

### Molecule Navigation

Finding your place:
  bd mol current              # Where am I?
  bd mol current gt-abc       # Status of specific molecule

Seamless transitions:
  bd close gt-abc.3 --continue   # Close and advance
  bd close gt-abc.3 --no-auto    # Close but don't auto-claim next

The old way (3 commands):
  bd close gt-abc.3
  bd ready --parent=gt-abc
  bd update gt-abc.4 --status=in_progress

The new way (1 command):
  bd close gt-abc.3 --continue
